lesson.menu_title: Closing the Deal -- Confirming the Final Terms
lesson.menu_desc: Question tags (..., didn't we? ..., won't you?) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Closing the Deal
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael Pelizaro | Aula 20 -- Closing the Deal

listenings.text: A negotiation is not finished when both sides say yes; it is finished when the contract is signed. Last quarter we agreed on the cloud renewal, but the real work started after the handshake. First, I had to finalize the terms. I went through every line of the term sheet and checked it against what we had actually agreed: the price, the longer term, the uptime guarantee, and the exit clause. Then I confirmed everything with the vendor before we put anything on paper. This is where question tags became my best tool. I would say, we agreed on the twelve-month term, didn't we? and, you will keep the ninety-nine point nine percent uptime, won't you? Each tag let me check one final detail and get a clear yes. Once both sides confirmed the same terms, our legal team could formalize the agreement and prepare it for signature. We set the effective date for the first of the month, and we planned a short kick-off call to start the new contract smoothly. The day the sign-off came through, I felt the deal was truly done. So remember: you close the deal when you confirm the final terms, get the signature, and proceed to the kick-off. Until then, nothing is final.

Hi Rafael, this is Sofia from procurement. Congratulations, the cloud contract is officially closed. Legal received the signed copy this morning, and the sign-off is complete. The effective date is the first of next month, exactly as you planned, and the kick-off call is on the calendar. The leadership team asked me to pass on a message: this is your twentieth project with us, and they say the way you finalize a deal, confirm every term, and get a clean signature has become the standard for the whole department. So here is the last request of this program. They would like you to write a one-page closing checklist that other managers can follow: finalize the term sheet, confirm the terms with question tags, agree the effective date, get the signature, and plan the kick-off. They said it should be ready before the next steering meeting. Thank you for everything, Rafael. You really know how to close a deal. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, I finalized the term sheet and checked every line against what we had agreed. Next, I confirmed the key terms with the vendor using question tags, like we agreed on the price, didn't we? Then our legal team formalized the agreement and prepared it for signature. After that, both sides completed the sign-off and we set the effective date. Finally, we planned a short kick-off call to start the new contract smoothly.
